description: This is an optical metallographic micrograph of the cross-section of Fe-7.5Al-0.65C alloy after 24 h isothermal oxidation in dry air at 900 °C, with a scale bar of 200 μm. The dark region on the top and left is the external environment/embedding material, while the light region is the etched alloy matrix with visible fine microstructural features. No oxide nodules or internal oxidation defects are observed at the oxidized surface, which is distinct from morphologies obtained at lower temperatures: internal oxidation occurs at 600 °C, abundant nodules form at 700 °C, and few small nodules exist at 800 °C. This observation confirms that sufficient Al atom mobility at 900 °C enables the formation of a continuous protective Al₂O₃ or spinel oxide scale, suppressing internal oxidation and iron-rich nodule growth, supporting the conclusion that the oxidation resistance of the alloy increases as oxidation temperature rises from 600 °C to 900 °C.
